result:
## Finding

The actual scan-trigger construction logic is **not** in `driver_nl80211.c` itself — that file only has a thin dispatch wrapper. The real work happens in `src/drivers/driver_nl80211_scan.c` (compiled/linked as part of the same driver module, sharing `driver_nl80211.h`). Worth flagging since the report pointed at the wrong file.

- `driver_nl80211.c:7223` `driver_nl80211_scan2()` — the `.scan` callback registered in `wpa_driver_nl80211_ops`; just forwards to `wpa_driver_nl80211_scan(bss, params)`, no error logic of its own.
- `driver_nl80211.c:455` `nl80211_cmd_msg()` — shared netlink-message allocator used by the scan-trigger path (and everything else).

## Error-path functions and their distinct failure conditions

**1. `nl80211_cmd_msg()` — `driver_nl80211.c:455`** (builds the base genl message; returns `NULL` on failure)
- `nlmsg_alloc()` fails (OOM)
- `nl80211_cmd()`/`genlmsg_put()` fails
- `nl80211_set_iface_id()` fails — `nla_put_u64(WDEV)` or `nla_put_u32(IFINDEX)` fails

**2. `nl80211_scan_common()` — `driver_nl80211_scan.c:106`** (builds the `NL80211_CMD_TRIGGER_SCAN`/`NL80211_CMD_START_SCHED_SCAN` message; returns `NULL` via `goto fail` → `nlmsg_free`)
- `nl80211_cmd_msg()` itself returned `NULL`
- `nla_nest_start(NL80211_ATTR_SCAN_SSIDS)` fails
- `nla_put()` of any requested SSID fails
- `nla_put(NL80211_ATTR_IE)` for `extra_ies` fails
- `nla_nest_start(NL80211_ATTR_SCAN_FREQUENCIES)` fails
- `nla_put_u32()` of any requested frequency fails
- (when `mac_addr_rand` set) `nla_put(NL80211_ATTR_MAC)` fails
- (when `mac_addr_mask` set) `nla_put(NL80211_ATTR_MAC_MASK)` fails
- `nla_put_u32(NL80211_ATTR_SCAN_FLAGS)` fails

**3. `wpa_driver_nl80211_scan()` — `driver_nl80211_scan.c:214`** (top-level trigger, `-1` on failure, `0` on success)
- `TEST_FAIL()` test-injection hook triggers → immediate `return -1`
- `nl80211_scan_common()` returned `NULL` → `return -1`
- (P2P probe path) `nla_nest_start(NL80211_ATTR_SCAN_SUPP_RATES)` fails → `goto fail`
- (P2P probe path) `nla_put()` of the 2.4 GHz rate mask fails → `goto fail`
- (P2P probe path) `nla_put_flag(NL80211_ATTR_TX_NO_CCK_RATE)` fails → `goto fail`
- `send_and_recv_msgs()` (the actual `NL80211_CMD_TRIGGER_SCAN` netlink round-trip) returns nonzero — the kernel/driver rejected the scan request — and then:
  - if not `(drv->hostapd && is_ap_interface(drv->nlmode))` → `goto fail`, propagates kernel's `ret` (this is the generic "scan trigger failed" path a normal station-mode failure hits)
  - if in AP mode, switching to station mode via `wpa_driver_nl80211_set_mode()` fails → `goto fail`
  - if switched to station mode but the recursive `wpa_driver_nl80211_scan()` retry also fails → restore old AP mode, `goto fail`
- `fail:` label → `nlmsg_free(msg); return ret;` — the single terminal error return point for all of the above

The generic "scan failed" message the user sees (`wpa_printf(MSG_DEBUG, "nl80211: Scan trigger failed: ret=%d (%s)", ...)` at `driver_nl80211_scan.c:258`) is logged specifically when `send_and_recv_msgs()` returns nonzero — i.e. the kernel refused `NL80211_CMD_TRIGGER_SCAN` — distinct from the earlier `NULL`-message build failures, which fail silently with no matching debug print.
